name: Los Argüellos Gorges Loop
name_original: Boñar – circular por Reserva de la biosfera de Los Argüellos
description: This difficult 41.9-mile road cycling route through Los Argüellos Biosphere Reserve offers challenging climbs and stunning mountain scenery.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Boñar traverse a mountainous landscape within the province of León, Spain. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, offering routes that pass through the Picos de Europa Regional Park and border the Mampodre National Reserve. Cyclists encounter diverse natural features, including the large Porma Reservoir, the Porma River, and the extensive Pardomino Forest. This topography provides varied terrain for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Boñar The most popular road cycling route is Los Argüellos Gorges Loop, a 41.9 miles (67.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route traverses the Los Argüellos Biosphere Reserve. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Nocedo Waterfall – Hoces de Valdeteja loop from Barrio de las Ollas, a difficult 44.5 miles (71.6 km) path. This route passes by the Nocedo Waterfall, offering scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Boñar – Porma Reservoir loop from La Losilla, a 55.6 miles (89.5 km) trail leading through the Embalse del Porma, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Boñar is defined by mountainous terrain, the Porma Reservoir, and the Porma River. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Boñar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 35 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Boñar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Boñar?
A: Boñar offers a wide range of road cycling routes, with over 45 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult paths, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Boñar?
A: The mountainous terrain of Boñar, nestled within the Picos de Europa Regional Park, makes spring and autumn particularly scenic for road cycling. Autumn is especially stunning due to the vibrant colors of the Pardomino Forest. Summers are also popular, but be prepared for potentially warmer temperatures and plan your rides accordingly.

Q: What are the typical terrain and elevation challenges for road cycling in Boñar?
A: Road cycling in Boñar is characterized by significant elevation changes, as the region is surrounded by mountains and borders the Picos de Europa Regional Park. You can expect routes with challenging climbs and descents, offering panoramic views. For example, the route connecting Boñar with Puerto San Isidro features over 1,800 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Boñar?
A: While many routes in Boñar feature challenging terrain, there are options suitable for beginners. For instance, there is at least one easy road cycling route available, and 26 routes are rated as moderate. These routes often follow flatter sections along the Porma River or around the reservoir, providing a gentler introduction to the region's cycling.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs around Boñar?
A: Boñar is an excellent destination for challenging road cycling. Many routes feature substantial climbs, with 21 routes rated as difficult. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042283762/los-argueellos-gorges-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Argüellos Gorges Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 67.4 km trail with over 940 meters of elevation gain, traversing the Los Argüellos Biosphere Reserve. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14989599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boñar – Porma Reservoir loop from La Losilla&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 89.5 km with over 1,100 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Boñar?
A: Yes, Boñar offers several circular road cycling routes that allow you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042283762/los-argueellos-gorges-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Argüellos Gorges Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14989599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boñar – Porma Reservoir loop from La Losilla&lt;/a&gt;, both providing a comprehensive tour of the region's landscapes.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along road cycling routes in Boñar?
A: Road cyclists in Boñar can enjoy diverse natural features and landmarks. Routes often pass through the Picos de Europa Regional Park and border the Mampodre National Reserve, offering spectacular mountain vistas. You might encounter the expansive Porma Reservoir, the lush Pardomino Forest, and the Porma River. Keep an eye out for viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Rejos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for breathtaking panoramas.

Q: Is there a route that passes by the Nocedo Waterfall?
A: Yes, there is a specific route that takes you past the stunning Nocedo Waterfall. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31858165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nocedo Waterfall – Hoces de Valdeteja loop from Barrio de las Ollas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 71.6 km path that offers scenic views and the chance to experience this beautiful natural attraction, also known as Cola de Caballo Waterfall.

Q: Can I cycle around the Porma Reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The Porma Reservoir (Embalse del Porma) is a central feature for road cycling in the region. A popular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14989599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boñar – Porma Reservoir loop from La Losilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is 89.5 km long and offers mountain views and glimpses of abandoned villages along its shores. A shorter, difficult 32.7 km route also circles the reservoir.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Boñar?
A: While many routes in Boñar are challenging, families can find suitable options, particularly on the easier or moderate sections. Look for routes with less elevation gain, possibly along the Porma River or specific, shorter loops around the Porma Reservoir. Always check the difficulty grade and distance to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What are the parking options for road biking in Boñar?
A: Boñar, as a hub for outdoor activities, generally offers parking facilities within the town and at popular trailheads. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking areas, especially when starting a route from a specific village or attraction point.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Given the region's popularity with outdoor enthusiasts, many villages and towns along the routes, such as Puebla de Lillo, Riaño, and Sabero, offer cafes, restaurants, and local establishments where you can stop for refreshments. Boñar itself has a variety of options. Planning your route with potential stops in mind is recommended.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Boñar?
A: The road cycling routes in Boñar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 35 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning mountainous landscapes, the diverse natural features like the Porma Reservoir and Pardomino Forest, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs that offer spectacular panoramic views.
tips: Small mountain town located in the north of the province of León on the banks of the Porma river. The town is a tourist destination and gateway to the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park and the Picos de Europa National Park.
Valdepiélago is a small town next to the Curueño river and the entrance to the Curueño gorge. Spectacular views of the jagged edge mountain rock formation as the road angles along the river gorge.
Las Hoces de Vegacervera is a protected natural space in the north of León. 12 kilometer path towards Matallana on the banks of the Torío river with spectacular panoramic views of the river gorge.
Palace of Renaissance architecture from the mid-15th century in Otero de Cureño. The notable feature of the Palace is a round tower.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Boñar – circular by Los Argüellos Biosphere Reserve cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route typically takes around 3 hours and 15 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult and requires very good fitness. It covers approximately 67.4 kilometers with an elevation gain of 940 meters. The terrain features mostly well-paved surfaces, but as with many hard road rides in mountainous regions, be prepared for significant climbs and descents, and potential variations in surface quality on some segments.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in Los Argüellos Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for general cycling within the Los Argüellos Biosphere Reserve. However, always respect local regulations and private property during your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring or autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the potential for snow or very cold conditions in winter, which can make cycling in this mountainous region challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the circular route in Boñar?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Boñar, you can usually find parking within the town itself. Look for public parking areas or street parking that allows for a full day's stay.

faqs.question: What natural highlights and landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning natural beauty within the UNESCO Los Argüellos Biosphere Reserve. You'll encounter deep canyons like the Curueño Gorge and Vegacervera Gorge, pristine rivers, and quaint stone villages. Keep an eye out for the Palacio de los Álvarez-Acevedo and the charming water features, fountains, and bridges around Boñar.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: No, this road cycling route is not suitable for dogs. It involves cycling on public roads with varying traffic conditions and is designed for human cyclists. It would be unsafe for a dog to accompany you on this type of ride.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including the Camino Olvidado (Puente Almuhey-La Robla) and the Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect in Los Argüellos Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: You can expect dramatic high-mountain scenery with deep canyons, pristine rivers, and expansive pastures. The Biosphere Reserve is known for its exceptional biodiversity, so you might spot a variety of flora and fauna characteristic of the Leonese mountains. The area also features charming stone and slate villages.

faqs.question: Are there any places to stop for food or drinks along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through several villages, it's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially given the route's difficulty and length. Boñar, as the starting and ending point, offers options for cafes and pubs.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: For this difficult road cycling route, you should bring a well-maintained race bike, appropriate cycling gear, a helmet, sufficient water and nutrition, a repair kit (including spare tubes and a pump), a first-aid kit, and navigation tools. Given the mountainous terrain, layers of clothing are advisable, and always check the weather forecast before you go.
